title: verbascum urobracteum
intro: Verbascum urobracteum, also known as the Upland Mullein or Hoary Mullein, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Verbascum urobracteum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
